chunk_summary: Guidelines for discharge nutrition prescriptions in persistent diarrhea, emphasizing measurable plans, micronutrient supplementation, follow-up schedules, and caregiver education to prevent malnutrition.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of measurable discharge nutrition plans for persistent diarrhea.
Learn age-specific dosing for Vitamin A and iron supplementation during recovery.
Identify follow-up schedules and stop rules for diet progression in malnourished children.
Educate caregivers on preventing iatrogenic malnutrition during diarrhea management.
key_facts: Vitamin A dosing: 50,000 IU for infants <6 months, 100,000 IU for 6–12 months, and 200,000 IU for >12 months as a single dose.
Iron supplementation: Start after acute diarrhea ends, at 3 mg/kg/day elemental iron when tolerated.
Target intake: Expressed as kcal/kg/day for under-5 programs, with planned feed frequency and ORS for ongoing losses.
Follow-up: Reassess within 48–72 hours for dehydration or weight loss; otherwise within 7 days for diet-stage decisions.
Stop rules: Escalate diet stage or refer if no improvement after 7 days; return precautions include blood in stool, vomiting, reduced urine output, edema, or lethargy.
reference_quote: Persistent diarrhea requires measurable nutrition plans, including kcal/kg/day targets, micronutrient supplementation, and caregiver scripts to prevent malnutrition. (IAP Standard Treatment Guidelines: Persistent diarrhea, Chapter 030)
document_key_topics: Discharge nutrition prescription for persistent diarrhea
Follow-up and stop rules for diet progression
